name: BERTopic (Neural Topic Modeling)

performance.notes: Embedding generation is bottleneck; GPU accelerates embedding; HDBSCAN clustering then fast

agent_readiness.notes: Neural topic modeling using embeddings + UMAP + HDBSCAN. Modular: swap embedding models (sentence-transformers, OpenAI, Cohere), dimensionality reduction (UMAP, PCA), clustering (HDBSCAN, k-Means), and representation models (KeyBERT, LLM-based). Dynamic topic modeling over time. Online learning for streaming data. Zero-shot topic classification. Merge, reduce, or search topics. Visualization with Plotly. Significantly better coherence than LDA/NMF.

gotchas: HDBSCAN can create many small topics — min_topic_size tuning required
Embedding generation is slow without GPU for large corpora
UMAP is non-deterministic — results vary across runs
Many small documents can cluster poorly
best_when: Discovering topics in customer feedback, reviews, or research papers
Dynamic topic evolution over time periods
Zero-shot topic classification with known topic labels
avoid_when: Real-time topic classification (use zero-shot classifiers instead)
When exact topic reproducibility is required
summary: BERTopic is neural topic modeling using sentence embeddings + UMAP + HDBSCAN clustering. Modular pipeline: swap embedding models (sentence-transformers, OpenAI), dimensionality reduction, clustering, and topic representation (KeyBERT, LLM-based). Dynamic topic modeling over time, online learning, and zero-shot classification. Produces much more coherent topics than LDA/NMF. Visualization via Plotly. Best-in-class for modern text corpus analysis.
